name: ncp-frame-header
description: NCP fixed-header encode and decode. Default header is 4 bytes (1 type + 1 flags + 2-byte BE payload length); extended header is 8 bytes (1 type + 1 flags + 4-byte BE payload length + 2 reserved bytes). Flags layout (MSB→LSB): EXT | RSV | RSV | RSV | ENC | FINAL | T1 | T0. Encoding tier T1T0: 00=JSON, 01=MsgPack, 10/11=Reserved.

vectors.description: Default 4-byte header for an AnchorFrame (0x01) with Tier-1 JSON encoding, FINAL=1, ENC=0, EXT=0, payload_len=200. Flags = 00000100 = 0x04.
Default 4-byte header for a CapsFrame (0x04), Tier-2 MsgPack, FINAL=1, ENC=0, EXT=0, payload_len=65535 (max for default mode). Flags = 00000101 = 0x05.
Extended 8-byte header (EXT=1) for a StreamFrame (0x03) carrying a 1 MiB Tier-2 MsgPack chunk, FINAL=0 (mid-stream). Flags = 10000001 = 0x81. Reserved bytes (6,7) MUST be 0x0000.
Decode round-trip: parse a HelloFrame (0x06) header with default mode, Tier-1 JSON, ENC=0, FINAL=1, payload_len=312. Flags = 00000100 = 0x04.

Negative: header declares default mode (EXT=0) but real payload would be 70000 bytes (>65535). Sender MUST set EXT=1 or chunk via StreamFrame. Receiver detecting size > 65535 with EXT=0 MUST reject with NCP-FRAME-SIZE-EXCEEDED.
Negative: reserved tier 0b10 in T1T0 (former Tier-3 slot). Spec marks Tier-3 Reserved; receivers MUST reject. Flags = 00000110 = 0x06.
